creator: portmaster@bsdforge.com
text:
(In reply to John Marino from comment #18)
> Okay, thanks.  It wasn't perfect; you might want to check the difference
> from what I committed versus what you submitted on the Makefile.  From
> memory:
> 
> 1) The "Created by: " line was the same error I pointed out a few times: it
> has to reflect the original port creator, not the port reviver
> 2) You left ".include <bsd.ports.pre.mk>", ".include <bsd.ports.post.mk>"
> when only <options> and <bsd.ports.mk> was needed.  Worse, nothing was
> between those includes making the mistake very obvious
> 
> 3) Need to have tabs after variable definitions, not spaces, and you need to
> align at 2 tabs or 3 tabs.
> 
> 4) the WITHOUT_PSYCO definition was dangling (did nothing) so it was removed.
> 
Points well noted. Thank you!

> 
> Your appreciation is appreciated.  Not everyone does.  Some folk treat this
> as some kind of "duty" we're expected to perform.  Not every says thank you,
> so thank you!
No. Thank _you_. :))

All the best to you, John.

--Chris
